title: enneapogon eremophilus
intro: Enneapogon eremophilus, also known as the saltgrass or saltbush grass, is a perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues. Enneapogon eremophilus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal preparation methods.
